iOS Engineer — Swift / UIKit / AppKit

Muhammad Osama Nadeem

I build the parts of an app that make a canvas feel obedient under a finger — layers, gestures, precision.

Layers top → bottom
  • ⠿ Slogan z-4
  • ⠿ Intelligence z-3
  • ⠿ Artificial z-2
  • ⠿ Head Icon z-1

This is the actual interaction I built for a production canvas editor — drag a layer to reorder it. On the real app, this is powered by a UICollectionView reorder handler.

Project 01 — Canvas Engineering

A canvas that listens

At Content Arcade, I worked on Logo Maker | AI Design Creator — a multi-platform logo and graphic design app shipped on iOS, iPadOS, and a native AppKit build for Mac. My focus was the canvas itself: the controls a user drags, rotates, and stacks to build something that didn't exist a minute ago.

Logo Maker — iOS App Store Logo Maker — Mac App Store
Native AppKit — Mac

The same canvas, built natively for Mac

The Mac app isn't a Catalyst wrapper — it's a native AppKit build with its own layer panel, arc-text control, and spacing adjustment, sharing the same canvas logic as the iOS app.

Custom Control

An interactive sticker, rebuilt from scratch

I extended ZDStickerView into a custom UIKit control purpose-built for this app — handling rotation, resizing, spacing, and move gestures, plus arc text that curves cleanly around a path as you type.

  • Gesture-driven rotation & resize
  • Arc text along a custom path
  • Live font & spacing adjustment
Layer System

Reorder, sync, and keep it all mapped correctly

Users needed to control stacking order directly — I implemented layer reordering with UICollectionView, then built the cross-device sync that keeps a draft's layer positions accurately mapped between a user's iPad and Mac.

  • Drag-to-reorder layer panel
  • Cross-device draft sync (iOS ↔ Mac)
  • Accurate positional mapping across platforms
Text Controls

Editing in place, without breaking flow

Text editing happens directly on the canvas — controls for font, size, and translation sit inline, so adjusting a headline never means leaving the drawing surface.

Project 02 — Modules That Serve Real Traffic

the Entertainer — Hotel World Wide

At the Entertainer, I led development of the Hotel World Wide module — the destination browsing, offers, and hotel-detail flow shown here — built on a modular architecture and now serving roughly 2,000 users a month.

Browse & Discover

Destinations, offers, and trending hotels

Destination shortcuts, limited-time offers, and trending hotels — all built as reusable, modular components shared across the wider app.

Offer Redemption

Hotel detail & monthly offers

Hotel detail pages surface Buy-1-Get-1 offers with redemption rules and validity windows — one of the core flows driving repeat use of the module.

Project 03 — UI From Zero

Gathers

A dating app built from a blank canvas in a three-month sprint — I led the UI/UX from scratch and implemented MVVM-R architecture from the first commit, working directly with application directors and creative leads to keep execution aligned with a fast-moving product vision.

Gather Labs — Nov 2022 to Jan 2023 — team of 5

Also Built

ConsoliAds

SDKs other developers rely on

Designed and shipped a distributable ad-mediation framework adopted by external developer teams, plus Unity plugins for game developers — and wrote the integration docs myself. Also introduced GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines during my time at the Entertainer to speed up the team's release cycle.

Stack

Swift UIKit AppKit SwiftUI Objective-C MVVM VIPER CocoaPods CI/CD — GitHub Actions XCTest REST APIs Firebase
